name: Arr

unions.name: Expectation
unions.comment:
An expectation for the decoded array that hasn't been met.

  - `ExpectLength`: expected a different exact length
  - `LengthInBound`: expected a length within a range (→ see [`InNat.Expectation`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-bounded-nat/latest/InNat#Expectation))
      - `ExpectAtLeast` some minimum in a range
      - `ExpectAtMost` some maximum in a range

See [errorToString](Arr#errorToString) and the `serialize` functions.

aliases.name: Arr
aliases.comment:
An `Arr` describes an array where you know more about the amount of elements.


## types

  - amount >= 5

```
Arr (Min Nat5) ...
```

  - 2 <= amount <= 12

```
Arr (In Nat2 (Nat12Plus a_)) ...
```


### as argument types

  - = 15

```
Arr (Only Nat15) ...
```

  - amount >= 4

```
Arr (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4_) max_) ...
```

  - 4 <= amount <= 15

```
Arr (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4_) Nat15) ...
```


## as storage types

To store in your `Model` for example (which means: no type variables)

  - = 15

```
Arr (Only Nat15) ...
```

  - amount >= 4

```
Arr (Min Nat4) ...
```

  - 4 <= amount <= 15

```
Arr (In Nat4 Nat15) ...
```
aliases.type: Typed.Typed Typed.Checked Internal.ArrTag Typed.Internal (Internal.Content length element)

aliases.name: Error
aliases.comment:
An error for when a decoded array doesn't have the expected length.

You can transform it into a message with [`errorToString`](Arr#errorToString).

See the `serialize` functions.
aliases.type: { expected : Arr.Expectation, actual : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } }

values.name: all
values.comment:
Whether all elements satisfy a test.

    Arr.all isEven (Arr.from2 2 4)
    --> True

    Arr.all isEven (Arr.from2 2 3)
    --> False

    Arr.all isEven Arr.empty
    --> True
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Arr.Arr length_ element -> Basics.Bool

values.name: any
values.comment:
Whether any elements satisfy a test.

    Arr.any isEven (Arr.from2 1 3)
    --> True

    Arr.any isEven (Arr.from2 1 2)
    --> False

    Arr.any isEven Arr.empty
    --> False
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Arr.Arr length_ element -> Basics.Bool

values.name: at
values.comment:
The element at a valid position in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from4 0 1 2 3
        |> Arr.at nat1 FirstToLast
    --> 1

    Arr.from4 0 1 2 3
        |> Arr.at nat1 LastToFirst
    --> 2
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) maxLength_) element -> element

values.name: dropWhen
values.comment:
Remove values where a condition is met.

    Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5
        |> Arr.dropWhen (\n -> n < 3)
    --> Arr.from3 3 4 5
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat5Plus a_)) number_
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength_ maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 maxLength) element

values.name: empty
values.comment:
No elements.

    Arr.empty
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 atLeast0_) element
        |> InArr.push ":)"
    --> : Arr (In Nat1 (Nat1Plus atLeast0_)) String
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 atLeast0_) element_

values.name: errorToString
values.comment:
Convert an error to a readable message.

    { expected = Arr.ExpectLength nat11
    , actual = { length = nat10 }
    }
        |> Arr.errorToString
    --> "Expected an array of length 11, but the actual length was 10."

(example doesn't compile)
values.type: Arr.Error -> String.String

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Reduce an `Arr` in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.fold FirstToLast (++) ""
    --> "live"

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.fold LastToFirst (++) ""
    --> "evil"

    sum =
        Arr.fold FirstToLast (+) 0

    product =
        Arr.fold FirstToLast (*) 0
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> result -> result) -> result -> Arr.Arr length_ element -> result

values.name: foldWith
values.comment:
A fold in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) where the initial result is the first value in the `Arr`.

    Arr.foldWith FirstToLast maximum
        (Arr.from3 234 345 543)
    --> 543

    Arr.foldWith LastToFirst (++)
        (Arr.from3 "m" "l" "e")
    --> "elm"
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> element -> element) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1_) maxLength_) element -> element

values.name: from1
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 1 element.
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat1 (Nats.Nat1Plus a_)) element

values.name: from10
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 10 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat10 (Nats.Nat10Plus a_)) element

values.name: from11
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 11 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat11 (Nats.Nat11Plus a_)) element

values.name: from12
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 12 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat12 (Nats.Nat12Plus a_)) element

values.name: from13
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 13 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat13 (Nats.Nat13Plus a_)) element

values.name: from14
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 14 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat14 (Nats.Nat14Plus a_)) element

values.name: from15
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 15 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat15 (Nats.Nat15Plus a_)) element

values.name: from16
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 16 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat16 (Nats.Nat16Plus a_)) element

values.name: from2
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 2 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat2 (Nats.Nat2Plus a_)) element

values.name: from3
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 3 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat3 (Nats.Nat3Plus a_)) element

values.name: from4
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 4 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat4 (Nats.Nat4Plus a_)) element

values.name: from5
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 5 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat5 (Nats.Nat5Plus a_)) element

values.name: from6
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 6 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat6 (Nats.Nat6Plus a_)) element

values.name: from7
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 7 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat7 (Nats.Nat7Plus a_)) element

values.name: from8
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 8 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat8 (Nats.Nat8Plus a_)) element

values.name: from9
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 9 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat9 (Nats.Nat9Plus a_)) element

values.name: fromArray
values.comment:
Create an `Arr` from an `Array`.

As every `Array` has `>= 0` elements:

    Arr.fromArray arrayFromSomewhere
    --> : Arr (Min Nat0)

Don't use it this way:

    Arr.fromArray
        (Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ])
    --> big no

Tell the compiler if you know the amount of elements. Make sure the it knows as much as you!

    Arr.from7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6
    --> ok

    Arr.nats nat7
    --> big yes
values.type: Array.Array element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) element

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Short for `Array.fromList list |> Arr.fromArray`. Create an `Arr` from a `List`.

As every `List` has `>= 0` elements:

    Arr.fromList listFromSomewhere
    --> : Arr (Min Nat0)

Don't use it this way:

    Arr.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ]
    --> big no!

Tell the compiler if you know the amount of elements. Make sure the it knows as much as you!

    Arr.from7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6
    --> ok

    Arr.nats nat7
    --> big yes
values.type: List.List element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) element

values.name: fromMaybe
values.comment:
`Arr.from1` if `Just`, `empty` if `Nothing`.

    Arr.fromMaybe (Just "hi")
    --> Arr.from1 "hi"
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat1Plus a_))

    Arr.fromMaybe Nothing
    --> Arr.empty
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat1Plus a_))
values.type: Maybe.Maybe element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 (Nats.Nat1Plus a_)) element

values.name: groupsOf
values.comment:
Split the `Arr` into equal-sized chunks in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

  - `groups`: the Arr divided into equal-sized Arrs
  - `less`: values to one side that don't fill a whole group

```
Arr.from7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
    |> Arr.groupsOf nat5 FirstToLast
--> { groups =
-->     Arr.from1 (Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5)
-->     : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a_))
-->         (Arr (In Nat5 (Nat5Plus b_)) number_)
--> , remaining =
-->     Arr.from2 6 7
-->     : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat5Plus c_)) number_
--> }

Arr.from7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
    |> Arr.groupsOf nat5 LastToFirst
--> { groups = Arr.from1 (Arr.from5 3 4 5 6 7) : ...
--> , remaining = Arr.from2 1 2 : ...
--> }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (Nats.Nat1Plus minGroupSizeMinus1) maxGroupSize groupSizeIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength_ maxLength) element -> { groups : Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 maxLength) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minGroupSizeMinus1) maxGroupSize) element), remaining : Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 maxGroupSize) element }

values.name: length
values.comment:
The amount of elements.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3 |> Arr.length
    --> nat3 : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat3Plus a_))

    between3And5Elements |> Arr.length
    --> : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat5Plus a_))

    atLeast3Elements |> Arr.length
    --> : Nat (Min Nat3)
values.type: Arr.Arr length element_ -> Nat.Nat length

values.name: lowerMinLength
values.comment:
Use a lower minimum length in the type.

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
    ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `Arr (Min Nat3) ...`

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
        |> Arr.lowerMinLength nat3
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn newMinLength minLength newMinIfN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In newMinLength maxLength) element

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every element.

    aToZ : Arr (In Nat26 (Nat26Plus a_)) Char
    aToZ =
        Arr.nats nat26
            |> Arr.map (val >> inABC)

    inABC index =
        ('a' |> Char.toCode)
            + index
            |> Char.fromCode

`val` refers to [`Typed.val`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typed-value/latest/Typed#val).
values.type: (element -> mappedElement) -> Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length mappedElement

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine the elements of 2 `Arr`s into new elements.
If one list is longer, its extra elements are not used.

    teamLives aBoard bBoard =
        Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lives + b.lives)
            aBoard
            bBoard

The length of all `Arr`s must be in the same range.

  - If 1 `Arr`'s maximum length isn't known:

```
Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lives + b.lives)
    (aBoard |> Arr.toMin)
    (bBoard |> Arr.toMin)

aBoard : Arr (In Nat2 someMax_) Field
bBoard : Arr (In Nat2 otherMax_) Field
```

  - If 1 `Arr` has a higher minimum length:

```
Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lives + b.lives)
    (aBoard |> Arr.lowerMinLength nat2)
    bBoard

aBoard : Arr (In Nat5 Nat10) Field
```
values.type: (a -> b -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: map3
values.comment: Combine the elements of 3 `Arr`s into new elements. Works like [map2](Arr#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length c -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: map4
values.comment: Combine the elements of 4 `Arr`s into new elements. Works like [map2](Arr#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length c -> Arr.Arr length d -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: minNats
values.comment:
Increasing natural numbers until below a given value.

Use [nats](Arr#nats) if you know the maximum length:

    Arr.nats nat5

    Arr.nats between2And10

If you don't:

    Arr.minNats atLeast10
    --> : Arr (Min Nat10)
    -->     (Nat (Min Nat10))

    from first length =
        Arr.minNats length
            |> Arr.map (MinNat.add first)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 max))

values.name: nats
values.comment:
Increasing natural numbers until below a given value.

    Arr.nats nat4
    --> Arr.from4 (Nat 0) (Nat 1) (Nat 2) (Nat 3)
    --> : Arr
    -->     (In Nat4 (Nat4PLus a))
    -->     (Nat (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a)))

    Arr.nats between2And10
    --> : Arr
    -->     (In Nat2 (Nat10Plus a))
    -->     (Nat (In Nat0 (Nat9Plus a)))

    from first length =
        Arr.nats length
            |> Arr.map (InNat.add first)

If you want to use a `Nat` as the length, but you don't know the maximum, e.g.

    Arr.nats (Nat.intAtLeast nat5 someInt) -- error

use [minNats](Arr#minNats).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min (Nats.Nat1Plus maxMinus1) ifN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min (Nats.Nat1Plus maxMinus1)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 maxMinus1))

values.name: order
values.comment:
Keep the order if [`FirstToLast`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/LinearDirection#LinearDirection),
reverse if [`LastToFirst`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/LinearDirection#LinearDirection).

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> Arr.order LastToFirst
    --> Arr.from3 3 2 1

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> Arr.order FirstToLast
    --> Arr.from3 1 2 3
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length element

values.name: random
values.comment:
Generate a given amount of elements and put them in an `Arr`.

    Arr.random nat5 (Random.float 0 1)

    --> : Random.Generator (Arr (In Nat5 (Nat5Plus a_)) Float)
    Nat.random
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLength maxLength ifN_) -> Random.Generator element -> Random.Generator (Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element)

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
An `Arr` with a given amount of same elements.

    Arr.repeat nat4 'L'
    --> Arr.from4 'L' 'L' 'L' 'L'

    Arr.repeat atLeast3 'L'
    --> : Arr (Min Nat3) Char
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element

values.name: replaceAt
values.comment:
Set the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat2 FirstToLast "confusion"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "am" "confusion"

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat1 LastToFirst "feel"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "feel" "ok"

An index that's out of bounds is ignored.

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat3 LastToFirst "feel"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
values.type: Nat.Nat index_ -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length element

values.name: resize
values.comment:
Resize an `Arr` in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/), padding with a given value.

    Arr.resize LastToFirst nat4 0
        (Arr.from2 1 2)
    --> Arr.from4 0 0 1 2

    Arr.resize LastToFirst nat2 0
        (Array.from3 1 2 3)
    --> Arr.from2 2 3

    Arr.resize FirstToLast nat4 0
        (Array.from2 1 2)
    --> Arr.from4 1 2 0 0

    Arr.resize FirstToLast nat2 0
        (Array.from3 1 2 3)
    --> Arr.from2 1 2

This is a quick way to gain some type-level knowledge about the length.
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn newMinLength newMaxLength ifN_) -> element -> Arr.Arr length_ element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In newMinLength newMaxLength) element

values.name: restoreMaxLength
values.comment:
Make an `Arr` with a fixed maximum length fit into functions with require a higher maximum length.

While designing argument annotations as general as possible:

    atMost18Elements : Arr (In min_ Nat18) ...

The argument in `atMost18Elements` should also fit in `atMost19Elements`.

    atMost19Elements theArgument
    --> error :(

    atMost19Elements
        (theArgument |> Arr.restoreMaxLength nat18)
    --> works
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn max newMax ifN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min newMax) element

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Alias to `Arr.order LastToFirst`: flip the order of the elements.

    Arr.from4 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.reverse
    --> Arr.from4 "e" "v" "i" "l"
values.type: Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length element

values.name: take
values.comment:
A certain number of elements from a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.take nat7 FirstToLast
    --> Arr.from7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6

Use [`takeMax`](Arr#takeMax) if you don't know the exact amount of elements to take.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N taken atLeastTaken (Nat.Is minNotTaken_ Nat.To minLength) is_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In taken atLeastTaken) element

values.name: takeMax
values.comment:
A certain number of elements from a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.takeMax nat7 between3And7 FirstToLast
    --> : Arr (In Nat3 (Nat7Plus a_)) ...

The first number is the maximum taken amount. The second number is the amount of taken elements.

Use [`take`](Arr#take) if you know the exact amount of elements to take.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N maxTaken atLeastMaxTaken (Nat.Is minNotTaken_ Nat.To minLength) is_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minTaken maxTaken takenIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken atLeastMaxTaken) element

values.name: to1
values.comment:
Access the only value in the `Arr`.

    Arr.to1 (Arr.from1 "hi")
    --> "hi"
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat1) element -> element

values.name: to10
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T10`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat10) element -> Toop.T10 element element element element element element element element element element

values.name: to11
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T11`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat11) element -> Toop.T11 element element element element element element element element element element element

values.name: to12
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T12`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat12) element -> Toop.T12 element element element element element element element element element element element element

values.name: to13
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T13`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat13) element -> Toop.T13 element element element element element element element element element element element element element

values.name: to14
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T14`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat14) element -> Toop.T14 element element element element element element element element element element element element element element

values.name: to2
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T2`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat2) element -> Toop.T2 element element

values.name: to3
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T3`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat3) element -> Toop.T3 element element element

values.name: to4
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T4`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat4) element -> Toop.T4 element element element element

values.name: to5
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T5`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat5) element -> Toop.T5 element element element element element

values.name: to6
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T6`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat6) element -> Toop.T6 element element element element element element

values.name: to7
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T7`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat7) element -> Toop.T7 element element element element element element element

values.name: to8
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T8`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat8) element -> Toop.T8 element element element element element element element element

values.name: to9
values.comment: Transform the `Arr` into a `Toop.T9`. This makes accessing elements and pattern matching easier.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.Only Nats.Nat9) element -> Toop.T9 element element element element element element element element element

values.name: toArray
values.comment:
Convert the `Arr` to an `Array`.
Just do this in the end.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can.

    Arr.nats nat5
        |> Arr.map val
        |> Arr.toArray
    --> Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

`val` refers to [`Typed.val`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typed-value/latest/Typed#val).
values.type: Arr.Arr length_ element -> Array.Array element

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Short for `Arr.toArray arr |> Array.toList`. Convert the `Arr` to a `List`.
Just do this in the end.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can.

    Arr.nats nat5
        |> Arr.map val
        |> Arr.toList
    --> [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

`val` refers to [`Typed.val`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typed-value/latest/Typed#val).
values.type: Arr.Arr length_ element -> List.List element

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment: `Nothing` if the `Arr` is `empty`, else `Just` it's only value.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In min_ Nats.Nat1) element -> Maybe.Maybe element

values.name: toMin
values.comment:
Convert the `Arr (In min ...)` to a `Arr (Min min)`.

    between4And10Elements |> Arr.toMin
    --> : Arr (Min Nat4) ...

There is only 1 situation you should use this.

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1Element, between1And10Elements ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `Arr (Min Nat1) ...`

    [ atLeast1Element
    , between1And10Elements |> Arr.toMin
    ]
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min min) element

values.name: updateAt
values.comment:
Change the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) based on its previous value.

    Arr.from3 1 20 30
        |> Arr.updateAt nat0 FirstToLast ((*) 10)
    --> Arr.from3 10 20 30

    Arr.from3 1 20 30
        |> Arr.updateAt nat0 LastToFirst (\x -> -x)
    --> Arr.from3 1 20 -30

An index that's out of bounds is ignored.

    Arr.from3 1 20 30
        |> Arr.updateAt nat3 FirstToLast ((*) 10)
    --> Arr.from3 1 20 30
values.type: Nat.Nat index_ -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> element) -> Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length element

values.name: when
values.comment:
Only keep values that satisfy a test.

    Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5
        |> Arr.when (\n -> n >= 3)
    --> Arr.from3 3 4 5
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat5Plus a_)) number_
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength_ maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 maxLength) element

values.name: whenAllJust
values.comment:
If every `Maybe` is present, return all of the values. Return `Nothing` if any element is `Nothing`.

    Arr.empty |> Arr.whenAllJust
    --> Just Arr.empty

    Arr.from3 (Just 1) (Just 2) (Just 3)
        |> Arr.whenAllJust
    --> Just (Arr.from3 1 2 3)

    Arr.from3 (Just 1) Nothing (Just 3)
        |> Arr.whenAllJust
    --> Nothing

Funnily, this can sometimes even be nicer than `Maybe.mapN`:

    groupCall =
        Arr.from5 aUser bUser cUser dUser eUser
            |> Arr.map .phoneNumber
            |> Arr.whenAllJust

    -- vs
    groupCall =
        Maybe.map5 Toop.T5
            aUser.phoneNumber
            bUser.phoneNumber
            cUser.phoneNumber
            dUser.phoneNumber
            eUser.phoneNumber
values.type: Arr.Arr length (Maybe.Maybe value) -> Maybe.Maybe (Arr.Arr length value)

values.name: whenJust
values.comment:
Take every `Just value` & drop every `Nothing`.

    Arr.from3 (Just "This") Nothing (Just "fine")
        |> Arr.whenJust
    --> Arr.from2 "This" "fine"
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a_)) String

[Call `map` and then `whenJust` to get the same functionality as "filterMap"](https://github.com/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/blob/master/Q%20%26%20A.md#why-no-filtermap-but-only-whenjust).

    Arr.from3 "1.2" "2" "hello"
        |> Arr.map String.toInt
        |> Arr.whenJust
    --> Arr.from1 2
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a_)) Int
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength_ maxLength) (Maybe.Maybe value) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In Nats.Nat0 maxLength) value

name: InArr

aliases.name: Error
aliases.comment:
An error for when the length of a decoded array is different from an exact expected length. You can transform it into

  - a message: [`errorToString`](InArr#errorToString)
  - an [`Arr.Error`](Arr#Error): [`generalizeError`](InArr#generalizeError)

See [`serialize`](InArr#serialize).
aliases.type: { expected : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) }, actual : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } }

values.name: append
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `Arr` with an exact amount of elements to the right.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.append nat3 (Arr.from3 4 5 6)
    --> Arr.from6 1 2 3 4 5 6
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastAdded_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To minLengthSum) (Nat.Is maxLength Nat.To minSumMax)) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Only added) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthSum minSumMax) element

values.name: appendIn
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `Arr` which has multiple possible amounts to the right.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.appendIn nat3 nat5
            between3And5Elements
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 (Nat8Plus a))

Use [`append`](InArr#append) if the appended `Arr` has an exact amount of elements.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMin atLeastAddedMin_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To minLengthSum) addedMinIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMax atLeastAddedMax_ (Nat.Is maxLength Nat.To maxLengthSum) addedMaxIs_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In addedMin addedMax) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthSum maxLengthSum) element

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Elements after a certain number of elements in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from4 0 1 2 3
        |> InArr.drop nat2 LastToFirst
    --> Arr.from2 0 1

    between6And10Elements
        |> InArr.drop nat2 FirstToLast
    --> : Arr (In Nat4 (Nat10Plus a_)) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N dropped_ atLeastDropped_ (Nat.Is minTaken Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is maxTaken Nat.To maxLength)) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken maxTaken) element

values.name: errorToString
values.comment:
Convert an error into a readable message.

    { expected = { length = InNat.ExpectAtLeast nat11 }
    , actual = { length = nat10 }
    }
        |> MinArr.errorToString
    --> "Expected an array of length >= 11, but the actual length was 10."

(example doesn't compile)

Equivalent to

    error
        |> InArr.generalizeError
        |> Arr.errorToString
values.type: InArr.Error -> String.String

values.name: generalizeError
values.comment:
Convert its error to a [`Arr.Error`](Arr#Error): an error type that covers all cases where `Array` to `Arr` conversions can fail.

    import Serialize exposing (Codec)

    Serialize.mapError MinArr.generalizeError
    --> : Codec MinArr.Error a -> Codec Arr.Error a

Use this if you serialize `Arr (In X (XPlus a_))` together with `Arr (In A Z)`:

    Serialize.tuple
        (InArr.serialize nat10 ...)
        (InArr.serializeIn nat10 nat99 ...)
    --> error : `Codec`s have different custom errors

    Serialize.tuple
        (InArr.serialize nat10
            InArr.generalizeError ...
        )
        (InArr.serializeIn nat10 nat20
            identity ...
        )
    --> Codec
    -->     Arr.Error
    -->     ( Arr (In Nat10 (Nat10Plus a_)) ...
    -->     , Arr (In Nat10 (Nat20Plus b_)) ...
    -->     )

Note: There's also [`errorToString`](MinArr#errorToString).
values.type: InArr.Error -> Arr.Error

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Put an element in the `Arr` at a given index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from3 'a' 'c' 'd'
        |> InArr.insertAt nat1 FirstToLast 'b'
    --> Arr.from4 'a' 'b' 'c' 'd'

    Arr.from3 'a' 'c' 'd'
        |> Array.insertAt nat2 LastToFirst 'b'
    --> Array.from4 'a' 'b' 'c' 'd'
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLength indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minLength) (Nats.Nat1Plus maxLength)) element

values.name: intersperse
values.comment:
Place a value between all members.

To get the correct final length type, we need to give the current minimum and maximum length as an arguments.

    Arr.from3 "turtles" "turtles" "turtles"
        |> InArr.intersperse "on" nat3 nat3
    --> Arr.from5 "turtles" "on" "turtles" "on" "turtles"
values.type: element -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N minLength atLeastMinLength_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To (Nats.Nat1Plus minDoubleLengthMinus1)) minIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N maxLength atLeastMaxLength_ (Nat.Is maxLength Nat.To (Nats.Nat1Plus maxDoubleLengthMinus1)) maxIs_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minDoubleLengthMinus1 maxDoubleLengthMinus1) element

values.name: isLength
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length. Is it `LessOrEqualOrGreater`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    chooseFormation :
        Arr (In min Nat50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        case
            characters
                |> InArr.isLength nat7 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.Equal only7 ->
                SpecialAttack only7

            Nat.Less atMost6 ->
                Retreat atMost6

            Nat.Greater atLeast8 ->
                Fight atLeast8
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N (Nats.Nat1Plus nMinus1) atLeastN (Nat.Is a_ Nat.To (Nats.Nat1Plus nPlusAMinus1)) (Nat.Is valueToMax_ Nat.To maxLength)) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is minToValue_ Nat.To (Nats.Nat1Plus nMinus1))) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Nat.LessOrEqualOrGreater (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest nPlusAMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus nMinus1) atLeastN) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat2Plus nMinus1) maxLength) element)

values.name: isLengthAtLeast
values.comment:
Is its length `BelowOrAtLeast` as big as a `Nat`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    first5 :
        Arr (In minLength_ maxLength) element
        -> Maybe (Arr (In Nat5 maxLength) element)
    first5 arr =
        case arr |> MinArr.isLengthAtLeast nat5 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.Below _ ->
                Nothing

            Nat.EqualOrGreater atLeast5 ->
                Just atLeast5
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowerBound (Nats.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) (Nat.Is atLeastRange_ Nat.To maxLength) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is (Nats.Nat1Plus lowestToLowerBound_) Nat.To lowerBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Nat.BelowOrAtLeast (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerBound maxLength) element)

values.name: isLengthAtMost
values.comment:
Is its length `AtMostOrAbove` a given length?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    -- at least 3 and only up to 50 tags
    tag :
        Arr (In (Nat3Plus orHigherMin_) Nat50) String
        -> a
        -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags :
        Arr (In (Nat3Plus orHigherMin_) max)
        -> a
        -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        case
            array
                |> Arr.fromArray
                |> InArr.isLengthAtMost nat50 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.EqualOrLess atMost53 ->
                tag value atMost53 |> Just

            Nat.Above _ ->
                Nothing
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N upperBound atLeastUpperBound (Nat.Is (Nats.Nat1Plus greaterRange_) Nat.To maxLength) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is minToUpperBound_ Nat.To upperBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Nat.AtMostOrAbove (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastUpperBound) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus upperBound) maxLength) element)

values.name: isLengthInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is its length `BelowOrInOrAboveRange`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    chooseFormation :
        Arr (In minLength_ Nat50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        case
            characters
                |> InArr.isLengthInRange nat10 nat20 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.InRange between10And20 ->
                SpecialAttack between10And20

            Nat.BelowRange atMost9 ->
                Retreat atMost9

            Nat.AboveRange atLeast21 ->
                Fight atLeast21
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowerBound (Nats.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) (Nat.Is lowerBoundToUpperBound_ Nat.To upperBound) lowerBoundIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N upperBound atLeastUpperBound (Nat.Is upperBoundToMax_ Nat.To maxLength) upperBoundIs_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is minToLowerBound_ Nat.To lowerBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Nat.BelowOrInOrAboveRange (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerBound atLeastUpperBound) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus upperBound) maxLength) element)

values.name: prepend
values.comment:
Add elements of an `Arr` with an exact amount of elements to the left.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.prepend nat3 (Arr.from3 4 5 6)
    --> Arr.from6 4 5 6 1 2 3
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastAdded_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To minLengthSum) (Nat.Is maxLength Nat.To maxLengthSum)) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Only added) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthSum maxLengthSum) element

values.name: prependIn
values.comment:
Add elements of an `Arr` which has multiple possible amounts to the left.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.prependIn nat3 nat5
            between3And5Elements
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 (Nat8Plus a))

Use [`prepend`](InArr#prepend) if the appended `Arr` has an exact amount of elements.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMin atLeastAddedMin_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To minLengthSum) addedMinIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMax atLeastAddedMax_ (Nat.Is maxLength Nat.To maxLengthSum) addedMaxIs_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In addedMin addedMax) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthSum maxLengthSum) element

values.name: push
values.comment:
Equivalent to `insertAt nat0 LastToFirst`. Put a new element after all the others.

    between5And10Elements
        |> InArr.push "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 (Nat11Plus a_)) String
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minLength) (Nats.Nat1Plus maxLength)) element

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Kick an element out of the `Arr` at a given index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    removeLast between1And10Elements =
        between1And10Elements
            |> InArr.removeAt nat0 LastToFirst
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) (Nats.Nat1Plus maxLengthMinus1)) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthMinus1 maxLengthMinus1) element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s with a specific amount of elements.

    import Serialize exposing (Codec)

    serializeGameRow :
        Codec
            String
            (Arr (Only Nat10) GameField)
    serializeGameRow =
        Arr.serialize nat10
            -- if we just want a simple error string
            Arr.errorToString
            serializeGameField

The encode/decode functions can be extracted if needed.

    encodeGameRow : Arr (Only Nat10) GameField -> Bytes
    encodeGameRow =
        Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeGameRow

    decodeGameRow :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (Only Nat10) GameField)
    decodeGameRow =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeGameRow
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLength maxLength ifN_) -> ({ expected : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) }, actual : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } } -> error) -> Serialize.Codec error element -> Serialize.Codec error (Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element)

values.name: serializeIn
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s within a minimum & maximum length.
To serialize `Arr`s of an exact length, use [`serialize`](InArr#serialize).

    import Serialize exposing (Codec)

    serialize10To15Ints :
        Codec
            String
            (Arr (In Nat10 (Nat15Plus a_)) Int)
    serialize10To15Ints =
        InArr.serializeIn
            nat10
            nat15
            -- if we just want a simple error string
            InArr.errorToString
            Serialize.int

The encode/decode functions can be extracted if needed.

    encode :
        Arr (In (Nat10Plus minMinus10_) Nat15) Int
        -> Bytes
    encode =
        Arr.lowerMinLength nat10
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serialize10To15Ints

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (In Nat10 (Nat15Plus a_) Int))
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serialize10To15Ints

For decoded `Arr`s with a length outside of the expected bounds, the `Result` is an error message.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound minUpperBound lowerBoundIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minUpperBound maxUpperBound upperBoundIfN_) -> ({ expected : Arr.Expectation, actual : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } } -> error) -> Serialize.Codec error element -> Serialize.Codec error (Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLowerBound maxUpperBound) element)

name: MinArr

aliases.name: Error
aliases.comment:
An error for when a decoded array has a length that's less than the expected minimum length. You can transform it into

  - a message: [`errorToString`](MinArr#errorToString)
  - an [`Arr.Error`](Arr#Error): [`generalizeError`](MinArr#generalizeError)

See [`serialize`](MinArr#serialize).
aliases.type: { expected : { length : { atLeast : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } }, actual : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } }

values.name: append
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `Arr` to the right.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> MinArr.append nat3
            atLeast3Elements
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N minAdded atLeastMinAdded_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To minLengthSum) is_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minAdded maxAdded_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min minLengthSum) element

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Elements after a certain number of elements in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    atLeast6Elements
        |> MinArr.drop nat2 LastToFirst
    --> : Arr (Min Nat4) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N dropped_ atLeastDropped_ (Nat.Is minTaken Nat.To minLength) is_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken maxLength) element

values.name: errorToString
values.comment:
Convert the [serialization](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) error into a readable message.

    { expected = { length = { atLeast = nat11 } }
    , actual = { length = 10 }
    }
        |> MinArr.errorToString
    --> "Expected an array of length >= 11, but the actual length was 10."

(example doesn't compile)

Equivalent to

    error
        |> MinArr.generalizeError
        |> Arr.errorToString
values.type: MinArr.Error -> String.String

values.name: generalizeError
values.comment:
Convert its error to a [`Arr.Error`](Arr#Error): an error type that covers all cases where `Array` to `Arr` conversions can fail.

    import Serialize exposing (Codec)

    Serialize.mapError MinArr.generalizeError
    --> : Codec MinArr.Error a -> Codec Arr.Error a

Use this if you serialize `Arr (In ...)` together with `Arr (Min ...)`:

    Serialize.tuple
        (MinArr.serialize nat10 ...)
        (InArr.serializeIn nat10 nat99 ...)
    --> error : `Codec`s have different custom errors

    Serialize.tuple
        (MinArr.serialize nat10 ...
            |> Serialize.mapError MinArr.generalizeError
        )
        (InArr.serializeIn nat10 nat99 ...)
    --> Codec
    -->     Arr.Error
    -->     ( Arr (Min Nat0) ...
    -->     , Arr (In Nat0 (Nat99Plus a_)) ...
    -->     )

Note: There's also [`errorToString`](MinArr#errorToString).
values.type: MinArr.Error -> Arr.Error

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Put a new element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    atLeast5Elements
        |> MinArr.insertAt nat0 LastToFirst
            "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) String

    cons :
        element
        -> Arr (In minLength maxLength_) element
        -> Arr (Min (Nat1Plus minLength)) element
    cons =
        MinArr.insertAt nat0 FirstToLast
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLength indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (Nats.Nat1Plus minLength)) element

values.name: intersperse
values.comment:
Place a value between all members.

To get the correct final length type, we need to give the current minimum length as an arguments.

    atLeast3Turtles
        |> MinArr.intersperse "on" nat3
    --> "turtles" "on" "turtles" "on" "turtles" ...
    --> : Arr (Min Nat5) String
values.type: element -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N minLength atLeastMinLength_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To (Nats.Nat1Plus minDoubleLengthMinus1)) minIs_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min minDoubleLengthMinus1) element

values.name: isLength
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length. Is it `LessOrEqualOrGreater`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    convertUserArguments :
        String
        -> Result String (Arr (Only Nat3))
    convertUserArguments =
        let
            wordArr =
                String.words arguments
                    |> Arr.fromList
        in
        case
            wordArr
                |> MinArr.isLength nat3 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.Equal only3 ->
                Ok only3

            Nat.Less atMost2 ->
                Err
                    ("I need 3 arguments, but there are only "
                        ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length atMost2))
                        ++ "."
                    )

            Nat.Greater atLeast4 ->
                Err
                    ("I need exact 3 arguments, so "
                        ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length atLeast4))
                        ++ " is too much."
                    )
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N (Nats.Nat1Plus nMinus1) atLeastN (Nat.Is a_ Nat.To (Nats.Nat1Plus nPlusAMinus1)) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is minToValueMinus1_ Nat.To nMinus1)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Nat.LessOrEqualOrGreater (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest nPlusAMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus nMinus1) atLeastN) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (Nats.Nat2Plus nMinus1)) element)

values.name: isLengthAtLeast
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length.
Is it `BelowOrAtLeast` that value?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    convertUserArguments : String -> Result String (Arr (Min Nat3))
    convertUserArguments arguments =
        let
            wordArr =
                String.words arguments
                    |> Arr.fromList
        in
        case
            wordArr
                |> MinArr.isLengthAtLeast nat3 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.EqualOrGreater atLeast3 ->
                Ok atLeast3

            Nat.Below atMost2 ->
                Err
                    ("I need at least 3 arguments, but there are only "
                        ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length atMost2))
                        ++ "."
                    )
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound (Nats.Nat1Plus maxLowerBoundMinus1) ifN_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is lowestToMinLowerBound_ Nat.To minLowerBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Nat.BelowOrAtLeast (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest maxLowerBoundMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min minLowerBound) element)

values.name: isLengthAtMost
values.comment:
Is its length `AtMostOrAbove` a given length?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    -- only up to 50 tags
    tag : Arr (In min Nat50) String -> a -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags : Array String -> a -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        case
            array
                |> Arr.fromArray
                |> MinArr.isLengthAtMost nat50 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.EqualOrLess atMost50 ->
                tag value atMost50 |> Just

            Nat.Above _ ->
                Nothing
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minUpperBound maxUpperBound ifN_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To minLength) (Nat.Is lowestToMinUpperBound_ Nat.To minUpperBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Nat.AtMostOrAbove (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest maxUpperBound) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (Nats.Nat1Plus minUpperBound)) element)

values.name: prepend
values.comment:
Add elements of an `Arr` to the left.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> MinArr.prepend nat3
            atLeast3Elements
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N minAdded atLeastMinAdded_ (Nat.Is minLength Nat.To minLengthSum) is_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minAdded maxAdded_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min minLengthSum) element

values.name: push
values.comment:
Put a new element after the others.

    atLeast5Elements
        |> MinArr.push "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) String
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (Nats.Nat1Plus minLength)) element

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Kick out the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    removeLast atLeast1Element =
        atLeast1Element
            |> MinArr.removeAt nat0 LastToFirst
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (Nats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthMinus1 maxLength) element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s with a minimum amount of elements.

    import Serialize exposing (Codec)


    -- we can't start if we have no worlds to choose from
    serializeSaves : Codec String (Arr (Min Nat1) World)
    serializeSaves =
        MinArr.serialize nat1
            -- if we just want a simple error string
            MinArr.errorToString
            serializeWorld

The encode/decode functions can be extracted if needed.

    encode : Arr (In (Nat1Plus minMinus1_) max_) World -> Bytes
    encode =
        Arr.lowerMinLength nat1
            >> Arr.toMin
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeSaves

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (Min Nat1) World)
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeSaves
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLength max_ ifN_) -> ({ expected : { length : { atLeast : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } }, actual : { length : Nat.Nat (Nat.Min Nats.Nat0) } } -> error) -> Serialize.Codec error element -> Serialize.Codec error (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min minLength) element)
